channel.title: Ad News Feed
channel.description: 広告プラットフォーム最新情報の自動収集フィード

item.title: [Meta Ads] Powering AI, Strengthening the Grid: Innovation in Space Solar Energy and Long-Duration Storage
[Microsoft Ads] When discovery becomes decision: How AI Search is redefining customer intent
item.description: AI compresses the consumer journey. Discover how Bing Search shapes intent, validates options, and drives action at key decision moments.

item.title: [Google Ads] Use Claude to Create High Converting Landing Pages for Google Ads
item.description:
This is how we used Claude to create high converting landing pages for our Google Ads campaigns.
In this video I take you through the results and the process we used.
👉 Get Free Access to my "Keywords & SWOT Analysis Tool for Google Ads" here: https://www.definedigitalacademy.com/claude-toolkit
🤑

item.title: [Meta Ads] Why Meta Keeps Pushing Value Rules
item.description: Meta has spent years removing advertiser control over targeting and placements, so actively encouraging value rules seemed contradictory. But it makes sense when you see where things are headed. Jon explains how a new test version lets you bid based on custom audience labels for lead quality and cus

item.title: [General] Dominate Local SEO: The Ultimate Pest Control Keyword Strategy #shorts
item.description: Broad match keywords like 'pest control' are too generic. Focus on specific, local terms like 'pest control Columbia South Carolina' for better results. See how local service ads dominate search results. #LocalSEO #KeywordResearch #PestControl #DigitalMarketing #SmallBusiness

item.title: [General] デザイナーが広告運用やってみて感じたこと
item.description:
アナグラムでデザイナーをしている私が、広告運用の兼務を始めました。運用経験ゼロからのスタートです。 もちろん日々できることが増えている最中ですが、「兼務」によって得られたものは想像以上でした。この記事…

The post デザイナーが広告運用やってみて感じたこと first appeared on アナグラム株式会社.

item.title: [General] Consolidate Ads with the New Creative Workflow
[General] White Label SEO Client Retention: 17 Early Warning Signals, Proactive Check-Ins, and a Practical Churn Prevention Playbook
item.description: Client retention in white label SEO is rarely lost in one dramatic moment. Most accounts do not disappear because of a single bad month, one missed keyword target, or one awkward meeting. They leave because several small problems go unaddressed at the same time: expectations start to drift, commu

item.title: [General] Gatorade Brings Back “Is It In You?”: Inside the “Trust What’s Inside” Football Campaign With Pulisic, Vinícius Júnior and Julián Álvarez
item.description: Gatorade’s new global football campaign does two things at once. It revives one of the most recognizable lines in sports marketing, “Is It In You?”, and it translates that legacy for a younger generation of athletes through a very current football lens. The campaign, titled “Trust What’s Inside,”

item.title: [General] White Label SEO for Franchises: A Practical Guide to Location Page Architecture, Brand Consistency, and Scalable Citation Management
item.description: Franchise SEO looks straightforward from the outside. A brand has one name, one broad service set, and multiple locations. In practice, it is one of the most operationally complex forms of local search. Every location needs to be discoverable in its own market, but every location also has to rein

item.title: [General] How to Pitch White Label SEO to Existing Web Design and PPC Clients: Scripts, Packages, and an Agency Upsell Playbook
item.description: If you already serve clients through web design or PPC, you are sitting on the easiest SEO pipeline you will ever have. That is not because SEO is an easy sale. It usually is not. It is because trust is already in place. Your clients already know your team. They have already approved invoices. Th

item.title: [General] 15 Link Building Strategies That Still Earn High-Quality Backlinks in 2026
item.description: Link building is still one of the clearest ways to improve organic visibility, but the way it works has changed. A decade ago, the conversation centered on volume. Today, the conversation is about relevance, editorial trust, topic alignment, and whether a link exists because it genuinely helps a

item.title: [General] Robert Pattinson’s 1664 Blanc Campaign : Inside “Unquestionably Good Taste” and What It Means for Premium Beer Marketing
item.description: When 1664 launched its new global platform with Robert Pattinson at the center, it did more than add another celebrity to a beer campaign. It built a clear, usable brand idea around a question many marketers avoid because it is difficult to define: what does “good taste” actually mean? That frami

item.title: [General] Powerade “Power Your Fate” FIFA World Cup 26 Campaign: Full Breakdown of the Ad, Athletes, Strategy and Marketing Impact
item.description: When a global sports drink brand launches a World Cup campaign, the obvious expectation is spectacle. Big stars. big visuals. big media. Powerade’s new FIFA World Cup 26 platform, “Power Your Fate,” does include all of that. But the more interesting part is what sits underneath the production val

item.title: [General] Google Shopping Try On and The Devil Wears Prada 2: How Google’s Virtual Fitting Room Works and Why This Campaign Matters
item.description: Fashion marketing usually leans on aspiration. Product marketing usually leans on explanation. What makes Google Shopping’s collaboration with The Devil Wears Prada 2 notable is that it tries to do both at once. In the lead-up to the film’s May 1 theatrical release, Google Shopping used the world

item.title: [General] Aerie Realmakers Community: Creator Program Requirements, Perks, No-AI Rules, and What It Means for Brand Marketing
item.description: Aerie has turned a long-running brand belief into a formal creator structure. With the launch of the Aerie Realmakers Community, the intimates and lifestyle brand is doing more than inviting people to post about products. It is putting boundaries, expectations, rewards, and identity around what k

item.title: [General] Mercedes-Benz Vans for Small Businesses: Real Costs, Sprinter Benefits, Tax Deductions, and What the “Price of Success” Campaign Gets Right
item.description: Mercedes-Benz Vans did something smart with its “Price of Success” campaign. Instead of treating the van as the whole story, it put the spotlight on the people who actually make a van matter: small business owners, tradespeople, service operators, and local companies that turn long days into reve

item.title: [Meta Ads] Consolidate Ads with the New Creative Workflow
item.description: One of the primary benefits of the new creative workflow is that it allows you to consolidate your ads. While fewer ads isn't necessarily better, it can be. Read more at jonloomer.com/workflow.

item.title: [General] Get More Leads: Your Secret to More Reviews #shorts
item.description: Struggling to get reviews? If you're not running ads and rely on referrals, you might be missing out. Servicing clients and doing great jobs leads to more word-of-mouth and repeat business, naturally increasing your customer base and review count. #BusinessGrowth #CustomerReviews #MarketingTips #Lea

item.title: [General] Apple Watch and iPhone Health Campaign : How Apple’s Health App Connects Sleep, Heart Health, Cycle Tracking, and Privacy
item.description: Apple’s latest global health campaign does not try to persuade people with abstract claims about innovation or future possibilities. Instead, it stays rooted in a very familiar experience: too many opinions, too much noise, and not enough clarity. In “Your Health. In Your Hands.”, Apple frames th

item.title: [General] Marc Jacobs’ “The Scene” With Rachel Sennott: Why the Luxury Microdrama Campaign Matters for Fashion Marketing
item.description: Marc Jacobs has introduced a new kind of fashion campaign, one that feels less like a traditional advertisement and more like a compact piece of entertainment designed for the way people now watch, scroll, and share. Titled The Scene and written by and starring Rachel Sennott, the pre-fall 2026 c

item.title: [General] Smoothie King’s New ‘The Best Kind of Delicious’ Ads: What the Campaign Says About Nutrition Advice Overload, Menu Growth and Brand Strategy
item.description: Smoothie King’s latest national ad campaign arrives at a moment when nutrition content is everywhere, but confidence is not. Consumers now encounter diet tips, protein advice, ingredient warnings, and “healthy swaps” from search results, short-form videos, influencers, wellness creators, and comp

item.title: [General] OpenAI GPT-5.5 Explained: Benchmarks, Pricing, API Access, Safety, and GPT-5.5 vs GPT-5.4
item.description: OpenAI’s GPT-5.5 is not just another model update with a slightly higher benchmark score. It is a clearer statement of how OpenAI wants AI systems to be used in real work: less as one-turn chatbots and more as systems that can carry a task across multiple steps, multiple tools, and multiple forms

item.title: [General] Snapchat Place Loyalty on Snap Map: How Top Visitor Badges Work, What They Mean, and Why This Update Matters
item.description: Snapchat has added a new layer to Snap Map that turns repeat visits into a visible in-app status signal. The feature, called Place Loyalty, assigns Top Visitor badges to users who visit certain places often enough to rank near the top of that location’s visitors. On the surface, that sounds simpl

item.title: [General] Meta Expands Instagram Management APIs: What the New Instagram API Updates Mean for Brands, Creators, and Developers
item.description: Meta has expanded Instagram management APIs in ways that matter for three groups immediately: brands running partnership campaigns, creators who publish sponsored content through third-party tools, and developers building publishing, analytics, moderation, and reporting workflows around Instagram

item.title: [General] YouTube Reduces Push Notifications for Inactive Subscribers: What Creators Need to Know About Reach, Engagement, and Channel Growth
item.description: If your YouTube strategy has ever depended on subscribers clicking the bell, this update matters. YouTube is reducing mobile push notifications for certain subscribers who selected “All” notifications but have not watched or engaged with a channel for about a month. Those viewers are not being re

item.title: [General] Meta’s Instants App : How Instagram’s Standalone Disappearing-Photo Test Works, Where It’s Available, and Why It Matters
item.description: Meta is testing a new standalone app tied to Instagram called Instants, and the concept is easy to grasp: open straight to the camera, take a quick photo, send it to friends, and let it disappear after it is viewed or after a short window passes. On the surface, that sounds familiar because it is

item.title: [General] Meta Now Lets Parents See What Teens Ask Meta AI: How the New Insights Tool Works, What Parents Can See, and What It Means
item.description: Meta has added a new layer to its parental supervision system: parents who supervise Teen Accounts can now see the topics their teens have asked Meta AI about over the previous seven days. On the surface, that sounds simple. In practice, it marks an important shift in how one of the world’s large

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Adds Sponsored Stores to the Shopping Tab: What Retailers Need to Know Now
item.description: Google is showing a new store-level sponsored format inside the Shopping tab, and that matters because it changes the unit of visibility. For years, most advertisers have treated Google Shopping as a product-level environment. A shopper searches, Google matches the query to Merchant Center data,

item.title: [General] Boost Your Google Rankings with Review Velocity! #shorts
item.description: Focus on review velocity: how many new 5-star reviews you get monthly. Google favors businesses with high review counts, especially from local guides who add photos. This engagement drives higher rankings. #ReviewVelocity #GoogleMyBusiness #LocalSEO #BusinessGrowth

item.title: [Meta Ads] Meta Partners With AWS on Graviton Chips to Power Agentic AI

item.description: If you’ve seen a KitKat logo turned into “KatKit” and wondered whether it was a printing error, a one-off stunt, or part of something bigger, the short answer is simple: it was a deliberate campaign by KitKat Canada to introduce a new product called Mix-Ups. The execution was intentionally small

item.title: [General] Paramount+ App Redesign 2026: What the New Short-Form Video Feed Means for Streaming, Discovery, and Engagement

The interface reflected that behavior. Large title art, rows of shows, personalized carousels, and a home screen designed to p

item.title: [General] L’Oréal Paris and Ulta Beauty’s “Mother & ___” Campaign With Eva Longoria: What the 2026 Mother’s Day Beauty Retail Push Means

item.title: [General] B2B Marketing Beyond Lead Generation: 7 Tactics to Increase Qualified Pipeline, Buying-Group Engagement, and Revenue

item.title: [Google Ads] 3 new ways Ads Advisor is making Google Ads safer and faster
item.description: Three new agentic safety and policy features integrated into Ads Advisor will help protect and streamline your Google Ads account.

item.title: [Meta Ads] Breaking Ground on a New AI-Optimized Data Center in Tulsa, Oklahoma
[General] YouTube動画広告で「機能訴求」をやめたらCPAが1/4になった話
item.description:
YouTube動画広告、正直なところ不安でした。 僕には趣味として動画を作った経験こそあったものの、「広告として成果を出す動画」のノウハウはゼロ。加えてクライアント側にも過去のYouTube広告で苦戦…

The post YouTube動画広告で「機能訴求」をやめたらCPAが1/4になった話 first appeared on アナグラム株式会社.
item.category: アナグラム ブログ

item.title: [Google Ads] Google広告の適格性確認の通知が来たら？必要な書類や操作を3ステップで解説
item.description:
Google広告を運用していると、「適格性確認」の通知が来て「Googleから適格性確認のメールが届いたけれど、何をすればいいのかわからない…」 と悩むことがありませんか？ 専門用語が多く、複雑に感じて後回しにしがちです …

"Google広告の適格性確認の通知が来たら？必要な書類や操作を3ステップで解

item.title: [General] Meta Value Rules for Audiences Explained
[General] Adobe CX Enterprise – What Adobe’s Agentic AI Platform Means for Agencies, Brands, and Customer Experience in 2026
item.description: Adobe has introduced a new enterprise offering called Adobe CX Enterprise, and the launch matters for a simple reason: it shows how Adobe wants customer experience, marketing operations, content production, and AI-driven decisioning to work together in one system rather than across disconnected t

item.title: [General] Marriott Bonvoy and Visa FIFA World Cup 2026 Campaign: Haaland, Vinicius Júnior, Tickets, 1-Point Drops, and the Sleepover Suite
item.description: When Marriott Bonvoy and Visa announced their FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign featuring Erling Haaland and Vinicius Júnior, the headline was easy to understand. Two global brands. Two globally recognized footballers. One of the biggest sporting events in the world. But the real story is not simply t

item.title: [General] Amika’s “On Your Wavelength” Campaign – Rashuna Durham, SHADOW, the 4 Hero Products
item.description: There are plenty of beauty campaigns built around aspiration. Fewer are built around recognition. That is what makes amika’s “On Your Wavelength” campaign worth a closer look. Instead of asking audiences to admire a celebrity, decode a trend, or chase a polished fantasy, the campaign starts with

item.title: [General] Claude Design by Anthropic Labs: Features, Pricing, Use Cases, and How It Fits Alongside Figma and Canva
item.description: Anthropic has introduced Claude Design, a new product from Anthropic Labs that brings visual creation directly into the Claude workflow. The idea is simple, but important: instead of opening a traditional design tool first, a user can describe what they want in plain language and have Claude gene

item.title: [General] Inside Green Mountain Coffee Roasters’ “That’s Green Mountain Good” Campaign: Mascot, Message, Media Strategy, and What It Means for Coffee Marketing
item.description: Green Mountain Coffee Roasters has returned to a simpler, clearer message at a moment when coffee marketing is crowded with premium cues, lifestyle signaling, and polished celebrity-driven creative. Its new campaign, “That’s Green Mountain Good,” does not try to out-luxury the luxury players. Ins

item.title: [General] Venmo Stash – What Rachel Sennott and Jordan Firstman’s “Between Friends” Campaign Means for Everyday Spending
item.description: Venmo has spent years occupying a very specific place in daily life. For many users, it has been the app for splitting dinner, settling rent, paying friends back after a rideshare, or sending money with a joke in the memo line. That identity is strong, familiar, and culturally sticky. It is also

item.title: [General] 7-Eleven Store Closures in 2026: Why 645 Locations Are Closing or Converting as Food-Forward Expansion Moves Toward an IPO
item.description: 7-Eleven’s plan to close or convert 645 North American stores during fiscal 2026 is not a single-story headline. It is a footprint story, a food strategy story, a margin story, and an IPO story all at once. At first glance, the number is large enough to suggest retrenchment. But the fuller pictur

item.title: [General] Casper’s “Daymares” Campaign – How Orchard Turned Everyday Stress Into a Clearer Sleep Brand Message
item.description: Casper’s new “Daymares” campaign is one of those rare brand efforts that can be understood immediately and still reward a closer look. On the surface, the premise is simple: daytime life can feel absurd, awkward, noisy, cramped, frustrating, and occasionally exhausting. Night, ideally, should fee

item.title: [General] Adobe Digital Trends Report – What the Data Says About AI, Personalization, and Customer Experience
item.description: If there is one clear message running through the Adobe Digital Trends Report, it is this: customer expectations are rising faster than most organizations can operationalize change. The gap is no longer just about having the right channels, the right content, or the right technology stack. It is
